textByHash.08de2c90747b7dad0c53cccdcfdc66f2933432ef: Can I help ye with anythin' else?
textByHash.128587a49dacf1f13592089c7fe5000fb45593a5: Aye, I'll add it t' me collection!
textByHash.182115716ef24ca337134b0860a702be6b0bbd13: CAN YOU STOP INTERRUPTIN' ME TALE?
textByHash.242deab3ae19e63f2b76379ecb563656418b1ead: I woke up in a bed an' the Skipper started on me, tellin' me I ruined his ship an' I need t' pay him back.
textByHash.3bbe87075bdf2aecb44475ae0d2f43f58fed94fd: Lightnin' struck the boat, an' a piece of wood holdin' up the sail fell an' struck me on the 'ead.
textByHash.3e8a99edf8708438c62c5515e5ff3bc2277516f4: As if by magic, winds started a blowin' an' the storm started movin' in our direction.
textByHash.5943abfbd57d1d4df796e5bd4b378c56a6d7d3f8: Oh, a damp egg! Yes, please!
textByHash.596b8b888a9e7f16b4e1b7d6b0126a59df8c2a2f: Or if ye manage to get yer hands on one of them there fancy Spirity Angler outfits, it comes with a built- in rope harness.
textByHash.5a49449289bd517f2fc9c5b45694644678095c7b: Ah, I should'a realised you were a landlubber. We on the ship call them snappers, but you lot call 'em lobsters.
textByHash.6b9e8b285f15ab502494dd3a628c47777169d201: Fine, 'ere be me tale of misfortune.
textByHash.6fe85a906c4f4fca50362a3aa7325772ee70c7ec: Have a good'n. I know I won't.
textByHash.701ea8e216463e4a47999f516bd217d5b9f18704: After a couple o' days, we pulled up the anchor an' sailed closer t' the Karamjan coast. There is where the big'ns are.
textByHash.839b0f37da33157963896cc9623c6f88a7f1b525: When we arrived at the spot, the Skipper saw a lack of fish swimmin' in the waters.
textByHash.a3682e2fba363723ec29fd488ce3c84e1c62292e: Caught a load of manta ray an' snappers.
textByHash.a7517a57e796ad98fb2dcf7ef556e4f6c07d7218: You landlubbers... It be a shark.
textByHash.b56d1e2c7699c740d3a37159b616e5e53b1c9920: Sorry my friend, the Skipper has been grindin' me up the wrong way lately. If ye need anythin', talk t' Cap'ain Pudi over by the dock.
textByHash.d6eb88e1cf78aaa7d2be996d4e848542569d367a: Ye might find tying yourself t' somethin' using a rope t' be a life saver.
textByHash.dbd0580fce9db454337213f1ee1ee2a83afdbb3f: Hush! Don't let Timallus hear ye speak o' the damp egg crate. It'll set 'im off!
textByHash.e638ebc02efc25aa1f22490220f44c695879c64d: Nay, he usually is. He be upset about his ship, is all.
textByHash.e6cf6081e267e16187cf51709d0996c2287c0445: This is some comfort to be sure.
textByHash.e719bad32c9d96144c836183acb528f36af6fcda: Watch out for the waves. If ye get hit by that you could end up lost at sea.
textByHash.f474881f12449df8267f281a38120e4bf14505e0: That ungrateful Skipper. How dare he blame me for that freak storm?
textByHash.f8a9420552f5ba5bd524b99008bcd639292f647d: About a half moon ago, we set sail on our fortnightly fishin' trip. 'Twas smooth sailin' all the way t' our spot.
textByHash.fc6d0cd89ec8df512cf3e3ae0b891ff24476b570: I looked yonder onto the horizon an' noticed a storm was a brewin', so we adjusted our course accordin'.
